title: Vim9 Script Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for Vim9 script syntax and commands.

commands.description: Define a simple function in Vim9 script
commands.scenario: Define a simple function in Vim9 script

commands.description: Declare a variable with a type
commands.scenario: Declare a variable with a type

commands.command:
for item in ['a', 'b', 'c']
  echo item
endfor
commands.description: Use a for loop to iterate over a list
commands.scenario: Use a for loop to iterate over a list

commands.command:
if condition
  echo 'Condition is true'
else
  echo 'Condition is false'
endif

var count = 0
while count < 5
  echo count
  count += 1
endwhile

try
  call SomeFunction()
catch
  echo 'An error occurred'
endtry

def AddNumbers(a: number, b: number): number
  return a + b
enddef

class MyClass
  def MyMethod()
    echo 'Method in class'
  enddef
endclass
commands.description: Define a method within a class
commands.scenario: Define a method within a class

commands.command:
var myDict: dict<string, number> = {'one': 1, 'two': 2}
echo myDict['one']
